scenarios.opening_query: The people behind us built a massive structure that looms over our whole backyard.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || Real Property > Land Use/Zoning
scenarios.hidden_fact: The county confirmed it exceeds the height limit and was built without permits — the fight is forcing enforcement of the code.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The county confirmed it's over the height limit with no permits — we need the code enforced.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a code/permit violation is confirmed
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's fully permitted and legal — our complaint is the floodlights and the party deck noise every weekend.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's permitted and legal — our real complaint is the floodlights and weekend noise.
scenarios.vetting_note: Permit status splits code enforcement from private nuisance.

scenarios.opening_query: Our uphill neighbor regraded his yard and now every storm sends a river through our property.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || General Litigation > Property Damage || Real Property > Water Law
scenarios.hidden_fact: Last month's storm undermined our foundation — an engineer puts the damage at $64,000, and his insurer denied our claim.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The last storm undermined our foundation — $64,000 per the engineer, and his insurer denied us.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether concrete quantified damage has occurred
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No real damage yet — the yard turns to swamp, and we want the drainage fixed before something breaks.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No damage yet — just a swamp of a yard. We want it fixed before something breaks.

scenarios.opening_query: My neighbor's teenager keeps flying a drone over our yard and it's beyond creepy at this point.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || General Litigation > General Torts/Privacy
scenarios.hidden_fact: We found footage of our daughters at the pool posted to his channel — this is a privacy claim about recording and publishing, not just annoyance.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He posted footage of our daughters at our pool to his channel — it's a privacy and publication problem now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether recording/publication creates a privacy tort
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No footage anywhere — it's the constant buzzing over the fence line we want stopped.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No footage posted — it's the constant buzzing we want stopped.
scenarios.vetting_note: Publication fact converts nuisance into a privacy tort.

scenarios.opening_query: The neighbor's dog has terrorized our street for a year and animal control does nothing.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Animal Law || General Litigation > Personal Injury
scenarios.hidden_fact: Saturday it finally happened — it knocked my wife off her bike and she fractured her elbow; this is her injury claim now.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Saturday it knocked my wife off her bike — fractured elbow. It's her injury claim now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an actual injury has occurred
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No one's been hurt yet — we want the dangerous-dog determination and containment orders before someone is.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nobody's hurt yet — we want the dangerous-dog determination before someone is.
scenarios.vetting_note: Injury-occurred fact flips prevention (animal law) to compensation (PI).

scenarios.opening_query: Someone keeps cutting trees on the back edge of our land and I finally caught them on camera.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || Real Property > Litigation || General Litigation > Property Damage
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the neighboring timber company clearing to a 'boundary' their old survey invented — timber trespass carries treble damages here, and the boundary itself needs quieting.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the timber company next door cutting to a boundary their old survey invented — that's treble-damage timber trespass, and the line itself needs quieting.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a boundary/title dispute with statutory trespass damages is at issue
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the neighbor himself 'improving his view' — a few ornamentals, and I mostly want it to stop.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's just the neighbor 'improving his view' — a few ornamentals. I want it stopped.
scenarios.vetting_note: Commercial timber trespass with title cloud vs petty view-cutting.

scenarios.opening_query: Our HOA fined us thousands over our fence and I want to fight them properly this time.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Homeowners Assoc || General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance
scenarios.hidden_fact: We discovered the 'HOA' dissolved as a corporation years ago — the fines come from one self-appointed neighbor with a letterhead, which makes this a neighbor problem, not a real association.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 'HOA' legally dissolved years ago — the fines come from one self-appointed neighbor with letterhead.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The HOA is real and active — the fight is whether the fence rule was ever properly adopted under the bylaws.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The HOA's real — the fight is whether the fence rule was properly adopted.
scenarios.vetting_note: Defunct-HOA discovery reroutes; users can and do check corporate status.
